zh-tw.title: 食物笑話精選
zh-tw.description: 從諧音梗店名到夜市對話，收錄各種讓人邊笑邊餓的台味食物笑話

en.title: Food Jokes Collection
en.description: From pun-filled shop names to night market banter — bite-sized food jokes that'll make you laugh and crave a snack

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音梗店名文化參考

zh-tw.content:
朋友開了間飲料店叫「可不可」。
我問：「為什麼取這個名字？」
他：「客人問可不可以打折，我可以理直氣壯回他 — 不可以。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 LINE 群組分享，搭配抱怨被店家拒絕折扣的話題
variations: 也可以改成早餐店版本：店名「加不加」，客人問加蛋免錢嗎，老闆回 — 不加

en.content:
My friend opened a drink shop called "Can or Can't."
I asked why.
Him: "When customers ask if they can get a discount, the answer is right there in the name — they can't."
en.editorNote: Share in chats about petty business names
variations: Breakfast shop version: name it "Yes or No." Customer asks if eggs are free. Owner: "No."

items.sourceNote: 端午節 粽子擬人格式 — 2026-05-02 research log seed

zh-tw.content:
粽子對著人說：
「我卸下外衣以後，照樣光鮮亮麗。
不像你 — 一張嘴，就露餡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節前後在 IG 限動發，配粽子照超應景
variations: 也可以加一句：「你還是先把嘴閉好再說話吧」
對主管版本：「主管的承諾跟粽子一樣 — 包得很漂亮，打開全是餡」

en.content:
A zongzi says to a person:
"When I take off my wrap, I still look good.
Unlike you — open your mouth, and the filling spills out."
en.editorNote: Perfect for Dragon Boat Festival posts with a zongzi photo
variations: Boss version: a manager's promise is just like a zongzi — wrapped beautifully, but open it up and it's all filler

items.sourceNote: 珍奶 厭世格式 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼珍珠奶茶這麼受歡迎？
因為它有奶、有茶、有珍珠 — 比很多人的人生豐富多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一上班 LINE 群組吐槽用
variations: 也可以接：「至少珍珠每一顆都很扎實，不像我的人生鬆散」

en.content:
Why is bubble tea so popular?
It's got milk, tea, and pearls — already richer than most people's lives.
variations: Add: "At least every pearl is solid — unlike my life choices."

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「為什麼臭豆腐越臭越好吃？」
我：「因為它放下了包袱，做自己。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 可以接著聊「人生何嘗不是一塊臭豆腐」
variations: 延伸版：「臭豆腐告訴我們，被討厭也能很搶手」

en.content:
Friend: "Why does stinky tofu taste better the smellier it gets?"
Me: "Because it stopped caring what people think."
en.editorNote: Use as a follow-up to "life is just a piece of stinky tofu"
variations: Extension: "Stinky tofu teaches us — you can be polarizing and still be in demand."

items.sourceNote: 牛肉麵 厭世上班族 cross — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼牛肉麵的湯特別香？
因為它熬了一整夜 —
跟剛下班的我一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合加班後在限動發牛肉麵照
variations: 也可以接：「差別是它越熬越香，我越熬越爛」

en.content:
Why is beef noodle soup so flavorful?
It simmered all night —
same as me after work.
en.editorNote: Pair with a late-night noodle photo on stories
variations: Extension: "The difference is it gets better, I get worse."

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨：「妹妹今天加蛋還是加培根？」
我：「都加。」
阿姨：「昨天熬夜了喔？」
我：「妳怎麼看出來的？」
阿姨：「沒睡飽的人才會用兩種蛋白質補回去。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班族日常觀察，適合限動配蛋餅照
variations: 結尾改成：阿姨默默多送一杯豆漿，說「撐住」

en.content:
Breakfast shop auntie: "Egg or bacon today, sweetie?"
Me: "Both, please."
Auntie: "Pulled an all-nighter, didn't you?"
Me: "How can you tell?"
Auntie: "Only sleep-deprived people order two kinds of protein to compensate."
en.editorNote: Office worker observational humor — pair with an egg pancake photo
variations: Ending alt: Auntie quietly throws in a free soy milk and says, "Hang in there."

items.sourceNote: 雞排 考試文化 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼考完試一定要吃雞排？
因為書讀進去要靠記憶力，
雞排吃進去只要靠胃容量 —
至少胃比大腦聽話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 考試季學生群組分享必備
variations: 畢業季版本：「至少這四年，我的胃從沒讓我失望」

en.content:
Why do students always eat fried chicken after exams?
Memorizing facts depends on your brain.
Eating chicken just depends on your stomach —
and at least the stomach actually does what you tell it.
en.editorNote: Group chat fuel during finals week
variations: Graduation version: "In four years of college, my stomach never let me down."

zh-tw.content:
滷肉飯是台灣人的心靈療癒。
不管那天再怎麼難熬，
看到那碗淋上滷汁的飯 —
你就會原諒自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上厭世時 po 文用
variations: 也可以結尾改：「然後決定明天再厭世一次」

en.content:
Braised pork rice is Taiwan's soul food.
No matter how rough the day was,
the moment you see the gravy hit the rice —
you forgive yourself.
en.editorNote: Sunday-night post when the Monday dread sets in
variations: Alt ending: "…and then decide to dread tomorrow all over again."

zh-tw.content:
為什麼餃子永遠不會孤單？

因為它總是「成雙成對」的下水餃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節單身者反擊用，記得面無表情念出來
variations: 對單身者版本：「我跟餃子一樣，都是被一鍋同類包圍著卻覺得很孤單」

en.content:
Why are dumplings never lonely?

They always go into the pot in pairs — and never solo.
en.editorNote: Single-person comeback for Valentine's — deliver with a straight face
variations: Single-person version: "I'm like a dumpling — surrounded by my own kind in a pot, and still feel alone."

items.sourceNote: 鶴茶樓 諧音店名 文化

zh-tw.content:
經過一間飲料店叫「鶴茶樓」。
我跟朋友說：「來喝茶嘍！」
朋友：「我不渴。」
我：「沒關係，這店名你只要念出口，就等於下單了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣諧音店名文化代表，可搭配各種台味店名介紹
variations: 也可以加上「八曜和茶」「吾燕煮」等其他諧音店名延伸

en.content:
We passed a drink shop literally called "Crane Tea House" — but in Mandarin it sounds exactly like "let's go drink tea!"
The trick is: if you say its name out loud, you've already placed an order.
en.editorNote: Showcase of Taiwan's pun-shop-name culture — works as an intro to any "funny shop names" thread
variations: Extend with other classic pun shop names from Taiwan's homophone-loving food scene

items.sourceNote: 火鍋 天氣連結 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼下雨天大家都想吃火鍋？

因為人生已經夠濕了，
至少嘴巴裡的東西要熱的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 梅雨季 LINE 群組約飯用
variations: 冬天版本：「天氣已經夠冷，至少胃要先給自己暖一下」

en.content:
Why does everyone crave hot pot on rainy days?

Life is already wet enough —
at least keep your mouth warm.
en.editorNote: Use this to drum up rainy-day group dinners
variations: Winter version: "Weather's cold enough — at least your stomach should warm itself first."

items.sourceNote: 上班族 便當 厭世 — original

zh-tw.content:
上班族的便當有三種：
今天買的、昨天剩的、
以及「沒時間吃」的。

而第三種，永遠是賣得最好的。
variations: 也可以延伸：「第四種叫做『放在抽屜裡發現時已長毛』的」

en.content:
There are three kinds of office worker lunchbox:
today's, yesterday's leftovers,
and the "no time to eat it" one.

The third type is always the bestseller.
variations: Extension: "The fourth kind is the one you find in your drawer two weeks later, growing fur."

items.sourceNote: 鹹酥雞 攤販對話 — original

zh-tw.content:
鹹酥雞攤老闆：「九層塔要嗎？」
我：「要。」
老闆：「辣嗎？」
我：「微辣。」
老闆：「胡椒？」
我：「OK。」
老闆：「再加 30 元。」
我：「為什麼？」
老闆：「靈魂的代價。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 下班買鹹酥雞時 IG 限動配照片用
variations: 也可以結尾改：老闆嘆氣說「現在連靈魂都漲價了」

en.content:
Fried chicken vendor: "Basil?"
Me: "Yes."
Vendor: "Spicy?"
Me: "Mild."
Vendor: "Pepper?"
Me: "Sure."
Vendor: "That'll be thirty more."
Me: "For what?"
Vendor: "The price of soul."
en.editorNote: Caption for late-night street food photos
variations: Alt ending: vendor sighs — "Even soul has inflation these days."

items.sourceNote: 端午節 粽子派系 上班族 cross — 2026-05-02 research log

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「你今年端午吃哪一派的粽子？」
我：「中立派。」
同事：「中立派是什麼？」
我：「誰先送到我家，誰就是我心目中的正統。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節前一週，用來終結南北粽戰爭
variations: 也可以接：「我發現嘴巴塞滿粽子的時候，立場其實很容易改變」

en.content:
Coworker: "Which side are you on for zongzi this year — northern or southern?"
Me: "Neutral."
Coworker: "What does neutral mean?"
Me: "Whoever delivers to my house first wins my heart."
en.editorNote: Use the week before Dragon Boat Festival to end the regional zongzi war
variations: Add: "Turns out, with a mouthful of zongzi, your political stance becomes very flexible."
